item.title: Dungeon Inc.
item.description: Armor Chaotic Magic Character Creation Combat Employee of the Month Equipment Table Favors Injuries and Death Magical Training Magical Weapons Martial Training Mission Structure Personal Effects Personal Objectives Primary Colleagues Resource Die Salary Seniority Shields Specialist Training Stat Che...

item.title: The Dungeon
item.description: A huge tower sitting atop a mountain, with a league-wide base. From the foot of the mountain, it is impossible to see the top.

item.title: Chaotic Magic
item.description: The effects of Chaotic Magic are strongly felt within The Dungeon.

item.title: Seniority
item.description: There are no Levels in Dungeon Inc. only a Seniority Ranking.

item.title: Suspicion
item.description: Hosts are not supposed to help themselves to Client’s Items and other Items left around The Dungeon.

item.title: Personal Objectives
item.description: A personal objective is a narrative goal set by the players, with the goal of bringing Life to Dungeon Inc. outside of it’s pure mission structure.

item.title: Stat Check
item.description: Whenever your character is in danger, or attempts something risky, you roll a Stat Check. Roll a d20.

item.title: Resource Die
item.description: Some items (food, ammunition, favors, armor quality) deteriorate over time. We use resource die to represent that deterioration, indicated with a Δ.

item.title: Combat
item.description: The rules of Combat in Dungeon Inc. are designed to depict exciting action scenes, rather than offering a tactical mini-game.

item.title: Armor
item.description: Each type of armor has an associated armor die that absorbs damage as if it were hit points.
